title: RFC 9457 Problem Detail
description: Standard error response body conforming to RFC 9457 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs, STD 97). Returned by the global error handling middleware for all API error responses with Content-Type: application/problem+json.

type.description: A URI reference that identifies the problem type. For known domain errors, follows the pattern {base_url}/errors/{slug}. For unknown errors, set to 'about:blank' per RFC 9457 §3.1.

title.description: A short, human-readable summary of the problem type. SHOULD NOT change between occurrences of the same type (except for localization). Derived from the HTTP status text.

status.description: The HTTP status code generated by the server for this occurrence of the problem.

detail.description: A human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence of the problem. Sanitized in production — never contains stack traces or raw Go error strings.

instance.description: A URI reference that identifies the specific occurrence of the problem. Set to the request path (e.g., /users/123).

code.description: EXTENSION: Machine-readable error code. Stable across releases — safe for programmatic dispatch by API consumers.

request_id.description: EXTENSION: Correlation ID matching the X-Request-Id response header. Use when contacting support or searching logs.

errors.description: EXTENSION: Per-field validation failures. Only present when status is 400 and validation errors exist. Omitted otherwise.

field.description: The JSON field name (snake_case) that failed validation.

message.description: Human-readable description of the validation failure.
examples: username must be at least 3 characters
